    19‚Üí- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er93]` (ErdŇĎs, 1993) and `[PrRo99]` (Pr√∂mel‚ÄďR√∂dl).

    32‚Üí
    33‚Üí- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
    34‚Üí- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1031` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
    35‚Üí- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
    36‚Üí- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) referencing source papers `[Er93, p.340]` and `[PrRo99]`.
    37‚Üí
    38‚Üí### `answer()` usage
    39‚Üí
    40‚ÜíThe problem is phrased as a direct assertion in the docstring: "If G is a graph on n vertices with no clique and no independent set of size ‚Č• 10 log n, then G contains an induced regular subgraph on ‚Č• c log n vertices‚Ä¶" This is **Case 3** (direct assertion).
    41‚Üí
    42‚Üí- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper) ‚ÄĒ correct for a Case 3 assertion.
    43‚Üí- [x] The problem has been solved affirmatively (Pr√∂mel‚ÄďR√∂dl); the theorem states the positive assertion, consistent with Case 3 for a solved problem.
    44‚Üí- N/A ‚ÄĒ no `answer()` used, so quantifier-placement rule does not apply.
    45‚Üí

    47‚Üí
    48‚Üí- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
    49‚Üí- [x] Value is `research solved` ‚ÄĒ valid category (proved by Pr√∂mel‚ÄďR√∂dl but not formally verified).
    50‚Üí- [x] Not marked `research open` despite containing `sorry` ‚ÄĒ correct, since `sorry` is in the proof body of a `research solved` theorem (the proof exists but isn't formalized).
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üí## 8. AMS Attribute
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üí- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute (on the same attribute line as `category`).
    55‚Üí- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.
    56‚Üí- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
    57‚Üí- [x] No duplicate numbers.
    58‚Üí- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics). Appropriate for this graph-theoretic/Ramsey-type problem.
    59‚Üí
    60‚Üí## 9. Proof Bodies
    61‚Üí
    62‚Üí- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line form, acceptable per checklist ¬ß9).
    63‚Üí- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (no definitions present).
    64‚Üí- [x] No informal proof sketches included (problem is `research solved`, not `research open`).
    65‚Üí- [x] No `native_decide`.
    66‚Üí
    67‚Üí## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems
    68‚Üí
    69‚Üí- N/A ‚ÄĒ no variants present. The docstring mentions the stronger Pr√∂mel‚ÄďR√∂dl result but does not formalize it as a separate variant. This is acceptable; adding a variant for the stronger result would be a nice enhancement but is not required.
    70‚Üí
    71‚Üí## 11. Code Quality
    72‚Üí
    73‚Üí- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (continuation lines in the theorem statement use appropriate indentation; `sorry` is indented 2 spaces from `by`).
    74‚Üí- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ąÉ`, `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąą`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Ąē`, `‚Č•`, `‚ąß`, `‚ÜĎ`.
    75‚Üí- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
    76‚Üí- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
    77‚Üí- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
    78‚Üí- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.
    79‚Üí
    80‚Üí## 12. Build Verification
    81‚Üí
    82‚Üí- [ ] Not verified in this review (requires `lake build`).
    83‚Üí
    84‚Üí---
    85‚Üí
    86‚Üí## Formalization Assessment
    87‚Üí
    88‚ÜíThe formalization captures the problem faithfully:
    89‚Üí
    90‚Üí- **Clique-free / independent-set-free condition:** `G.CliqueFree ‚Ćą10 * Real.log (‚ÜĎn)‚ĆČ‚āä` and `GŠ∂ú.CliqueFree ‚Ćą10 * Real.log (‚ÜĎn)‚ĆČ‚āä` correctly encode "no clique and no independent set of size ‚Č• 10 log n" (complement clique = independent set).
    91‚Üí- **Induced regular subgraph:** `‚ąÄ v ‚ąą S, (S.filter (G.Adj v)).card = d` correctly states that every vertex in S has exactly d neighbors within S under G, making the induced subgraph on S a d-regular graph. Since `SimpleGraph.Adj` is irreflexive, v is never counted among its own neighbors ‚ÄĒ this is correct.
    92‚Üí- **Neither empty nor complete:** `d ‚Č• 1` (each vertex has at least one neighbor, so not an empty graph) and `d + 1 < S.card` (degree < |S| ‚ąí 1, so not a complete graph). Correct.
    93‚Üí- **Size bound:** `(S.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• c * Real.log (‚ÜĎn)` for some `c > 0`. Correct.
    94‚Üí- **Asymptotic formulation:** Uses `‚ąÉ N‚āÄ, ‚ąÄ n ‚Č• N‚āÄ` to handle the asymptotic nature of the claim. Correct.
    95‚Üí
    96‚Üí**Minor note:** `Real.log` is the natural logarithm. The constant 10 in the problem is tied to the choice of log base. This is a standard formalization choice and does not affect correctness since the problem's specific constant comes from the source.
    97‚Üí
    98‚Üí## Verdict
    99‚Üí
   100‚Üí**PASS** ‚ÄĒ All checkable items conform to the checklist. The formalization is clean, well-documented, and mathematically faithful.

file.content:
# Review: `deepmind/1031.lean`

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line (`import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`), immediately after the copyright block, separated by one blank line.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 1031`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1031`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (question of ErdŇĎs, Fajtlowicz, and Staton about induced regular subgraphs in graphs with small clique and independence number).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[Er93]` (ErdŇĎs, 1993) and `[PrRo99]` (Pr√∂mel‚ÄďR√∂dl).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1031 ... end Erdos1031`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos1031`).
- [x] `open scoped Topology Real` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A ‚ÄĒ no custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem named `erdos_1031` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` attributes on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) referencing source papers `[Er93, p.340]` and `[PrRo99]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem is phrased as a direct assertion in the docstring: "If G is a graph on n vertices with no clique and no independent set of size ‚Č• 10 log n, then G contains an induced regular subgraph on ‚Č• c log n vertices‚Ä¶" This is **Case 3** (direct assertion).

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper) ‚ÄĒ correct for a Case 3 assertion.
- [x] The problem has been solved affirmatively (Pr√∂mel‚ÄďR√∂dl); the theorem states the positive assertion, consistent with Case 3 for a solved problem.
- N/A ‚ÄĒ no `answer()` used, so quantifier-placement rule does not apply.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research solved` ‚ÄĒ valid category (proved by Pr√∂mel‚ÄďR√∂dl but not formally verified).
- [x] Not marked `research open` despite containing `sorry` ‚ÄĒ correct, since `sorry` is in the proof body of a `research solved` theorem (the proof exists but isn't formalized).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute (on the same attribute line as `category`).
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics). Appropriate for this graph-theoretic/Ramsey-type problem.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line form, acceptable per checklist ¬ß9).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (no definitions present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches included (problem is `research solved`, not `research open`).
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A ‚ÄĒ no variants present. The docstring mentions the stronger Pr√∂mel‚ÄďR√∂dl result but does not formalize it as a separate variant. This is acceptable; adding a variant for the stronger result would be a nice enhancement but is not required.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (continuation lines in the theorem statement use appropriate indentation; `sorry` is indented 2 spaces from `by`).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ąÉ`, `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąą`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚Ąē`, `‚Č•`, `‚ąß`, `‚ÜĎ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified in this review (requires `lake build`).

---

## Formalization Assessment

The formalization captures the problem faithfully:

- **Clique-free / independent-set-free condition:** `G.CliqueFree ‚Ćą10 * Real.log (‚ÜĎn)‚ĆČ‚āä` and `GŠ∂ú.CliqueFree ‚Ćą10 * Real.log (‚ÜĎn)‚ĆČ‚āä` correctly encode "no clique and no independent set of size ‚Č• 10 log n" (complement clique = independent set).
- **Induced regular subgraph:** `‚ąÄ v ‚ąą S, (S.filter (G.Adj v)).card = d` correctly states that every vertex in S has exactly d neighbors within S under G, making the induced subgraph on S a d-regular graph. Since `SimpleGraph.Adj` is irreflexive, v is never counted among its own neighbors ‚ÄĒ this is correct.
- **Neither empty nor complete:** `d ‚Č• 1` (each vertex has at least one neighbor, so not an empty graph) and `d + 1 < S.card` (degree < |S| ‚ąí 1, so not a complete graph). Correct.
- **Size bound:** `(S.card : ‚ĄĚ) ‚Č• c * Real.log (‚ÜĎn)` for some `c > 0`. Correct.
- **Asymptotic formulation:** Uses `‚ąÉ N‚āÄ, ‚ąÄ n ‚Č• N‚āÄ` to handle the asymptotic nature of the claim. Correct.

**Minor note:** `Real.log` is the natural logarithm. The constant 10 in the problem is tied to the choice of log base. This is a standard formalization choice and does not affect correctness since the problem's specific constant comes from the source.

## Verdict

**PASS** ‚ÄĒ All checkable items conform to the checklist. The formalization is clean, well-documented, and mathematically faithful.

content.text: The review verdict is **PASS**. No changes needed.
